description: Autonomous protocol for deciding when to use a library component vs creating a new one, and how to create new components that are FDS-compliant, delightful, and presentation-ready.

step1_searchLibrary.action: Search platform lookup JSON (flow-{platform}.lookup.json) by component name and functional purpose
step1_searchLibrary.result_found: → Use library component. STOP. Do not build custom.

step2_searchFallback.action: Search references/components-data.md (broader fallback list covering all 118 families)
step2_searchFallback.result_found: → Use it. Note it needs a JSON entry. STOP.

step3_checkRelatives.action: Check if a close relative in the library covers the need with a different prop combination
examples: Need 'inline alert' → use Alert / Passive with appropriate type prop
Need 'loading button' → use Button with State=Loading prop
Need 'icon-only button' → use Action Button component
step3_checkRelatives.result_found: → Use relative + document adaptation in build manifest. STOP.

step4_createNew.action: Create new component per FDS foundations
mandatory: Fetch visual context via Figma MCP if any related component exists
Follow BEM + token map pattern (4 steps — see SKILL.md)
Apply at least one micro-interaction from micro-interactions.json
Add to build manifest as 'New components'

visualContextProtocol.rule: Always attempt to get a visual reference before building. Never guess layout.
visualContextProtocol.step1: Check lookup JSON for figma.nodeId + meta.fileKey
visualContextProtocol.step2: Call Figma MCP get_screenshot(nodeId, fileKey) — primary
visualContextProtocol.step3: On fail: fetch figma.netlifyFallback URL from lookup JSON
visualContextProtocol.step4: On fail: call FDS MCP inspect_frame(nodeId) if available
visualContextProtocol.step5: On fail: tell the designer → 'Please share a screenshot of [component name] from the [Flow App V2 / Flow Web V2] Figma file. Node ID: [nodeId]. Alternatively open: [figma link]'
visualContextProtocol.never: Never guess visual layout from props alone. Always seek visual confirmation.

rules: Name describes function, not appearance — f-route-card not f-dark-wide-card
Use nouns for blocks — f-message-bubble, f-price-matrix
Never use layout words as names — f-left-panel, f-top-row are forbidden

mandatoryStructure.step1_HTML: BEM HTML — block (.f-name), semantic elements (__), state/variant modifiers (--)
mandatoryStructure.step2_tokenMap: All component variables defined on the block selector. Every visual property aliased to a brand token. Every state pre-mapped.
mandatoryStructure.step3_rules: CSS rules consume ONLY --f-{component}-* vars. Never reference --f-brand-* in rules.
mandatoryStructure.step4_modifiers: Modifiers ONLY reassign component vars already on the block. Never add new CSS properties in modifiers.
mandatoryStructure.step5_states: All states covered: default, hover (web only), focus-visible, active/pressed, disabled, loading (if async), error (if validating), success (if completing)
mandatoryStructure.step6_motion: At least one micro-interaction from micro-interactions.json. Token-sourced timing + easing. prefers-reduced-motion handled.

tokenMapTemplate.description: Minimum required token map for any new interactive component

accessibility.contrast_text: WCAG 2.1 AA — 4.5:1 minimum between text and background
accessibility.contrast_large: WCAG 2.1 AA — 3:1 for text >18px regular or >14px bold
accessibility.contrast_ui: WCAG 2.1 AA — 3:1 for all UI components and focus indicators
accessibility.touchTarget: Minimum 44×44px on all interactive elements (iOS HIG + WCAG 2.5.5)
accessibility.ariaRoles: All interactive elements have correct ARIA roles, labels, and states
accessibility.keyboard: Full keyboard operability — Tab, Enter/Space, Escape, arrow keys where applicable

tokenCompliance.noHardcodedHex: Zero hardcoded hex values — 100% token-sourced colors
tokenCompliance.noPxValues: Zero hardcoded px for spacing — all via --f-brand-space-* tokens
tokenCompliance.noHardcodedDuration: Zero hardcoded ms/s for animations — all via --f-brand-motion-duration-*
tokenCompliance.noBaseTokensInRules: base-tier tokens (--f-base-*) never appear in component rules
tokenCompliance.cssFallback: Every var() has a cssFallback: var(--f-brand-*, #fallback)

typography.noExternalFonts: Zero Google Fonts, Adobe Fonts, or CDN font links
typography.compositeShorthand: font: var(--f-brand-type-*) always — never individual font-size/weight/family
typography.approvedNames: Only token names from design-tokens.md §Typography Styles — no invented names

motion.oneMicroInteraction: At least one micro-interaction from micro-interactions.json applied
motion.tokenSourcingOnly: All durations and easings via motion tokens — never hardcoded
motion.gpuOnly: Only transform and opacity animated — never layout properties

platform.viewport_onWeb: Web components that scale must include Viewport variant handling
platform.arabicRTL: Text-containing components must not break at dir=rtl
platform.iconSizing: Icons are only 24px (ic_nav_) or 48px (ic_badge_) — never other sizes

uxPrinciples.affordance: Visual affordance must be clear without hover — color, shape, and size alone indicate interactivity
uxPrinciples.feedback: Every user action gets feedback within 100ms — if response takes longer, show a loading state immediately
uxPrinciples.forgiveness: Destructive actions require confirmation or undo. Never fire-and-forget destructive operations
uxPrinciples.consistency: New components behave identically to existing FDS components in the same category
uxPrinciples.progression: Complex interactions are progressively disclosed — default state is always the simplest
uxPrinciples.luxury: Qatar Airways is premium travel. Interactions feel confident, unhurried, and intentional. Never playful or cartoonish.
uxPrinciples.clarity: If a user needs to think for more than 2 seconds about what to do next, the design has failed
uxPrinciples.localization: English and Arabic are equal — every component must support both scripts without visual degradation

approved: Fluid typography with clamp() — use FDS type scale as min/max bounds to stay within system
Container queries for component-level responsiveness — better than media queries for reusable components
CSS scroll-driven animations for reveal effects (with prefers-reduced-motion fallback)
View Transition API for page transitions (progressive enhancement — graceful fallback if unsupported)
content-visibility: auto on long lists and off-screen sections — improves paint performance
CSS :has() for parent-based state styling — e.g. form:has(:invalid) to style entire form on error
Logical properties (padding-inline, margin-block) for automatic LTR/RTL support
avoid: Glassmorphism backdrop-filter blur on large surfaces — GPU-intensive, poor performance on mobile
Scroll jacking / overriding native scroll behaviour
Auto-playing video backgrounds — battery drain, motion sensitivity
Parallax on mobile — causes motion sickness for many users
Skeleton loading for content loading < 300ms — causes more anxiety than a spinner
Dark patterns — deceptive UI, pre-ticked boxes, hidden unsubscribe
Spring/elastic overshoot animations — too playful for Qatar Airways brand
3D perspective transforms — not part of FDS visual language
Excessive micro-interactions — one per component maximum

requiredFields.usedFor: One sentence describing the use case
requiredFields.closestFDSEquivalent: Nearest existing FDS component name, or 'None'
requiredFields.microInteractionsApplied: List of patterns from micro-interactions.json applied
requiredFields.figmaGapFlag: FDS gap: f-{name} — [what it does]. Candidate for DS team to formalise in Figma.

example.usedFor: Chat bubble for AI-generated responses in the assistant interface
example.figmaGapFlag: FDS gap: f-ai-message — AI chat response container. Candidate for design system.

escalationProtocol.iconGap: Flag: 'Icon gap: [description] — no FDS icon exists for this. Recommend raising with design team. Placeholder: ic_nav_[nearest-semantic-name]'
escalationProtocol.componentGap: Flag: 'FDS gap: f-[name] — [description]. Built on FDS foundations. Candidate for Figma formalisation.'
escalationProtocol.tokenGap: Flag: 'Token gap: No FDS token for [use case]. Using nearest: [token name]. Recommend raising with DS team.'
escalationProtocol.designerNeeded: Flag: 'Visual context needed — please share screenshot of [component] from [Figma file]. Link: [figma link]'
